src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc package

Submodules

src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc.IpcService module

class src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc.IpcService.IpcService(*, host: str, port: int)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Core IPC (Inter-Process Communication) service for the OSIR framework.

action(request: dict)[source]

Dispatches incoming JSON requests to the appropriate action.

Parameters:

request (dict) – The raw JSON request from the IPC client.

Returns:

A standardized response object or an OsirException.

Return type:

OsirIpcResponse

action_create_case(osir_ipc: OsirIpc)[source]

Handles database and filesystem creation for a new forensic case.

action_exec_module(osir_ipc: OsirIpc)[source]

Initializes a case monitor for specific module execution.

action_exec_profile(osir_ipc: OsirIpc)[source]

Initializes a case monitor for profile-based execution.

action_get_case_handler(osir_ipc: OsirIpc)[source]

Returns all handlers associated with a specific case.

action_get_handler_status(osir_ipc: OsirIpc)[source]
action_get_task_log(osir_ipc: OsirIpc)[source]

Queries the JSONL log files for specific task traces.

static consume_generator(g)[source]

Utility to consume a generator in a background thread. Used to execute module setup logic after returning the initial handler UUID.

host: str
listen()[source]

Main server loop that maintains the TCP socket.

Handles incoming connections and ensures the server automatically restarts in case of a critical failure.

model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

port: int
start()[source]

Launches the IPC listener in a dedicated background thread.

src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc.JsonSocket module

src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc.JsonSocket.recv_exact(conn, num_bytes)[source]

Ensures that exactly the requested number of bytes are read from the socket.

Parameters:

  • conn (socket.socket) – The active network connection.

  • num_bytes (int) – Total bytes to read.

Returns:

The complete byte buffer.

Return type:

bytes

src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc.JsonSocket.recv_json(conn)[source]

Receives and decodes a JSON object using a 4-byte big-endian header.

src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc.JsonSocket.send_and_receive(host, port, obj)[source]

High-level client utility to perform a synchronous request-response cycle.

src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc.JsonSocket.send_json(conn, obj, pydantic=False)[source]

Serializes and sends a JSON object with a size-prefixed header.

Parameters:

  • conn (socket.socket) – The connection to send data over.

  • obj (Any) – The object to serialize.

  • pydantic (bool) – If True, uses the model’s native JSON serialization to handle complex types like Paths or UUIDs.

static CASE_NOT_FOUND(case: str) → OsirIpcResponse[source]

static MODULE_VALIDATION_ERROR(error: str) → OsirIpcResponse[source]

src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc.OsirIpc module

class src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc.OsirIpc.OsirIpc(*, action: str, case_name: str | None = None, modules: list[str] | None = None, profile: OsirProfileModel | None = None, task_id: str | None = None, case_uuid: str | None = None, handler_id: str | None = None, modules_instance: list[OsirModuleModel] | None = None, case_path: Path | None = None)[source]

Bases: OsirIpcModel

Functional implementation of an IPC request within the OSIR IPC service.

modules_instance

A list of forensic modules model.

Type:

Optional[list[OsirModuleModel]]

profile

The validated profile object if a profile-based execution was requested.

Type:

Optional[OsirProfileModel]

case_path

The resolved absolute path to the investigation case directory.

Type:

Optional[Path]

__init__(**data)[source]

Initializes the IPC request and hydrates nested forensic components.

case_path: Path | None
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

modules_instance: list[OsirModuleModel] | None
profile: OsirProfileModel | None
classmethod transform_input_data(data: dict) → dict[source]

Pre-validation hook to transform input strings into complex models.

Parameters:

data (dict) – The raw input dictionary before Pydantic validation.

Returns:

The transformed data dictionary.

Return type:

dict

src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc.OsirIpcClient module

class src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc.OsirIpcClient.OsirIpcClient(*, host: str = 'master-master', port: int = 8989)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

The primary client-side interface for communicating with the OSIR Master service.

host

The hostname or IP address of the OSIR Master node. Defaults to ‘master-master’ (internal Docker networking).

Type:

str

port

The TCP port the IpcService is listening on. Defaults to 8989.

Type:

int

host: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

port: int
send(osir_ipc: OsirIpcModel) → str[source]

Serializes and transmits an IPC model to the Master node.

Parameters:

osir_ipc (OsirIpcModel) – The validated request object containing the action and required parameters.

Returns:

The JSON-formatted response string from the Master service.

Return type:

str

src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc.OsirIpcModel module

class src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc.OsirIpcModel.OsirIpcModel(*, action: str, case_name: str | None = None, modules: list[str] | None = None, profile: str | None = None, task_id: str | None = None, case_uuid: str | None = None, handler_id: str | None = None)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Standard data model for outgoing Inter-Process Communication (IPC) requests.

action

The command to execute (e.g., ‘exec_module’, ‘create_case’, ‘get_task_log’).

Type:

str

case_name

The human-readable name of the forensic case.

Type:

str, optional

modules

A list of module names to execute.

Type:

list[str], optional

profile

The name of a pre-defined forensic profile to run.

Type:

str, optional

task_id

The unique UUID of a specific Celery task for log retrieval.

Type:

str, optional

case_uuid

The database UUID associated with a case.

Type:

str, optional

handler_id

The unique identifier for a case execution monitor.

Type:

str, optional

action: str
case_name: str | None
case_uuid: str | None
handler_id: str | None
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

modules: list[str] | None
profile: str | None
task_id: str | None
class src.osir_service.osir_service.ipc.OsirIpcModel.OsirIpcResponse(*, version: float, status: int | None = 200, message: str | None = None, response: dict | None = {})[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Standardized response envelope returned by the OSIR Master service.

version

The current version of the OSIR framework for compatibility checks.

Type:

float

status

The HTTP-style status code (e.g., 200 for success, 400+ for errors).

Type:

int

message

A descriptive summary of the operation result.

Type:

str, optional

response

A container for the actual data payload (e.g., log traces, handler IDs, or case metadata).

Type:

dict, optional

message: str | None
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

response: dict | None
status: int | None
version: float
